mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market expansion, which fuels consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when UNRATE falls but PAYEMS stagnates, with Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain flat or negative despite rising INDPRO during recovery phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to tighten policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth consistently coincides with positive 20-day SPX returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.9 million units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of periods when HOUST drops below 1.9 million units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with a rising unemployment rate indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown, which depresses earnings forecasts and causes negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no predictive power when FEDFUNDS rises but UNRATE remains stable, with directional accuracy below 50% over 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic strength, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansions, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient in a rolling regression.
